title: helianthemum croceum var. tananicum
intro: Helianthemum croceum var. tananicum, also known as rockrose, is a perennial plant species native to the Canary Islands, characterized by its fragrant yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum croceum var. tananicum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
